what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG icon of an image file, using a document outline with folded corner plus a small circle and zig-zag image motif.
observations: Root SVG uses viewBox 0 0 24 24 with no fill and currentColor stroke.
Stroke styling is 1.5 width with round line caps and joins.
The document outline is drawn as a path from x5/y3 to x19/y21 with a folded top-right corner.
A circle at cx 9 cy 13 with radius 1.3 reads as an image sun or marker.
A lower path from M5 19 through points 9 15, 13 18, and 19 14 reads as image terrain or graph-like line.
standouts: Single inline SVG asset with compact path-only geometry.
Filename aligns with a file plus image pictogram.
conventions: Follows common outline-icon conventions: 24px grid, no fill, currentColor stroke, rounded caps and joins.
Uses geometric SVG primitives directly rather than referencing symbols or external assets.
Does not include width or height attributes, leaving sizing to consumers.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria attributes are present in the SVG itself.
notes: A reusable outline icon representing an image file, intended to inherit color and size from its rendering context.
questions: Should this icon be treated as decorative or receive an accessible label when used?
Is the stroke width of 1.5 part of a broader icon style token from this design system?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a file-image icon, and the SVG content depicts a folded document containing image-like marks.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a lightweight, themeable file-image icon for UI surfaces that need to represent image documents or uploads.
contribution: Contributes a compact currentColor SVG glyph for representing an image-bearing file in a fused design asset ledger.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 outline SVG icon showing a folded-corner document with image-style circle and zig-zag marks, styled with currentColor stroke.
intention_for_embedding: This was for a reusable UI icon that communicates an image file while inheriting visual styling from its context.
